subject: Fermi GRB190530.43 Global MASTER-Net observations report
submitter: Vladimir Lipunov at Moscow State U/Krylov Obs  <lipunov@xray.sai.msu.ru>
body:
V. Lipunov, E. Gorbovskoy, V.Kornilov, N.Tyurina, P.Balanutsa, A.Kuznetsov, 
V.Vladimirov, D. Vlasenko, D.Zimnukhov, V.Senik, T.Pogrosheva, D.Kuvshinov 
(Lomonosov Moscow State University, SAI, Physics Department),

R. Podesta, C.Lopez, F. Podesta, C.Francile 
(Observatorio Astronomico Felix Aguilar OAFA),

H.Levato 
(Instituto de Ciencias Astronomicas, de la Tierra y del Espacio ICATE),

R. Rebolo, M. Serra 
(The Instituto de Astrofisica de Canarias)

D. Buckley 
(South African Astronomical Observatory),

O.A. Gres, N.M. Budnev, O.Ershova 
(Irkutsk State University, API),

A. Tlatov, D. Dormidontov 
(Kislovodsk Solar Station of the Pulkovo Observatory),

V. Yurkov, A. Gabovich, Yu. Sergienko 
(Blagoveschensk Educational State University)




MASTER-Tunka robotic telescope  (Global MASTER-Net: http://observ.pereplet.ru, Lipunov et al., 2010, Advances in Astronomy, vol. 2010, 30L)  located in Russia (Applied Physics Institute, Irkutsk State University) started inspect of the Fermi GRB190530.43 (trigger No 580904353,  7h 47m 40.80s , +33d 57m 00.00s, R=1) errorbox  15845 sec after trigger time at 2019-05-30 14:43:13 UT, with upper limit up to  17.0 mag. Observations started at twilight.  The observations began at zenit distance = 67 deg. The sun  altitude  is -10.9 deg. 

MASTER-IAC robotic telescope  located in Spain (IAC Teide Observatory) started inspect of the Fermi GRB190530.43 errorbox  37305 sec after trigger time at 2019-05-30 20:40:53 UT, with upper limit up to  18.6 mag. Observations started at twilight.  The observations began at zenit distance = 55 deg. The sun  altitude  is -10.0 deg. 

The galactic latitude b = -60 deg., longitude l = 98 deg.


Real time updated cover map and OT discovered available here: 
https://master.sai.msu.ru/site/master2/observ.php?id=1034101

We obtain a following upper limits.

The observation and reduction will continue. 
The message may be cited.
